title: 17-year-old drowns in Fall River pond
author: John Waller
bullets: A 17-year-old boy drowned at South Watuppa Pond in Fall River on Sunday night after struggling in the water.
State Police and Fall River police responded to a 911 call around 9:30 p.m., but initially could not locate the teen.
The body was found by a dive team at 11:35 p.m.; foul play is not suspected but the incident is under investigation.

title: Ranking Bruins’ top 10 prospects: Who stands out in Boston’s growing pipeline?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, Dean Letourneau, and Will Zellers lead Boston Bruins' prospect pool, with Hagens set for a bigger role in 2026-27.
Nils Bartholdsson, Alexander Command, and Chris Pelosi headline Boston's emerging talent, showcasing strong performances in various leagues.
Michael DiPietro, named AHL MVP, is set for more NHL opportunities after Bruins traded Joonas Korpisalo to give him a chance.

title: One of the few remaining privately owned Berkshire ‘cottages’ sold for $4 million
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The historic Pine Needles estate in Lenox, dating to 1903, sold for $4 million in June.
One of the last privately owned Berkshires cottages, it spans 80 acres with a 13,261 sq ft Colonial Revival home.
The new buyers intend to maintain Pine Needles as a family residence, preserving its residential heritage.

title: Body found in river near Brockton water treatment plant, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man's body was found floating near the Brockton water treatment plant, reported by police on Thursday.
Massachusetts State Police recovered the body, which was sent to the Medical Examiner's office for identification.
This is the third body found near the plant since 2024; investigations by Plymouth County DA's office are ongoing.

title: Man arrested for alleged assault of rideshare passenger at Logan Airport
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Leonard Bacon, 23, of Lowell, arrested for alleged assault on rideshare passenger at Logan Airport, causing serious bodily injury.
Incident occurred before 5:30 a.m. Friday; victim entered Terminal C with injuries claiming assault by a rideshare driver.
Bacon faces assault and battery charges, identified as a stabbing; scheduled to appear in East Boston District Court.

title: WBZ meteorologist returns after taking 3 years away to care for her son with cancer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: WBZ-TV meteorologist Sarah Wroblewski returns to work after nearly three years away caring for her son with cancer.
Her 6-year-old son, Declan, diagnosed with stage 4 brain cancer, is now out of active treatment and progressing.
Wroblewski will deliver weekend weather forecasts and has resumed some weekday coverage as Declan attends school and camp.

title: 2 killed in crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth town line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people, Tristan Bedient and Kate Aldrich, died in a car crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth Friday.
The crash involved a Toyota Camry and a Saab sedan, resulting in non-life-threatening injuries for the Saab's occupants.
Authorities are actively investigating the crash with involvement from Massachusetts State Police and local police departments.

title: Bus driver strike ends in Merrimack Valley after nearly 2 weeks
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit resumes service after a 10-day strike by over 130 drivers and mechanics.
The new one-year agreement includes a $4 hourly wage increase and maintains Saturday overtime for workers.
The strike ended with a shorter contract compromise, emphasizing future negotiations on weekend work schedules.

title: 5 takeaways as red-hot Red Sox rally past Mets, 3-2, for 9th straight victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox have won nine consecutive games, including a 3-2 victory over the New York Mets.
The team has allowed two runs or fewer in six consecutive games, showcasing strong pitching performances.
The Red Sox completed their first sweep of a nine-game road trip since 1977, entering the All-Star break on a high note.

title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan inducted into Fenway Music Hall of Fame after four consecutive nights at Fenway Park.
Kahan's mom announced his induction, joining acts like Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and the Zac Brown Band.
Kahan's Fenway shows featured fireworks and appearances by local sports stars Rob Gronkowski and David Ortiz.
